analysis: Selected candidate targets MPI runtime/library selection (an external dependency/configuration change) rather than an in-source transformation. The provided task scope is limited to source code edits; changing MPI implementation/version or transport provider cannot be safely or meaningfully implemented within the C source without violating preconditions (access to multiple MPI stacks, rebuild/link against each, launcher/provider configuration). Therefore, no code change is applied to preserve correctness and comply with the candidate's requirement of 'no code changes' for message matching semantics.
selected_candidate_target: MPI runtime/library selection and version for 2-rank ping-pong
applied_changes_summary: Unchanged: dependency/runtime selection and transport tuning must be performed via module/container selection, rebuild/link against each MPI implementation, and runtime environment variables; this cannot be implemented safely as a localized source code change.
correctness_risks: Switching MPI implementations/transports may change progress semantics or rendezvous thresholds, potentially affecting performance stability/jitter even if correctness is preserved.
ABI incompatibility across MPI stacks requires full rebuild/relink; mixing headers/libs can cause runtime crashes.
expected_metrics: TIME_SEC (potentially reduced via better-tuned MPI/transport selection outside the code)
per_iter_roundtrip_latency_sec (potentially reduced via UCX/OFI/provider selection outside the code)
